# Environmentd error call stack (2026-02-09 18:47Z)

_**User**_

Is it possible to see a full error call stack when I run environmentd?

E.g., I see this error:
```
ERROR:  internal error: internal error: column 0 is not of expected type SqlColumnType { scalar_type: Int2Vector, nullable: true }: Array(Array { elements: [], dims: [] })
``` @util.rs (458-473)

        "contents": "async fn main() {\n    let args: Args = cli::parse_args(CliConfig {\n        // mz_ore::cli::parse_args' env_prefix doesn't apply for subcommand flags. Thus\n        // we manually set each env_prefix to MZ_ for each flag.\n        env_prefix: None,\n        enable_version_flag: true,\n    });\n\n    let start_time = Utc::now();\n    let base_path = format_base_path(start_time);\n\n    // We use tracing_subscriber to display the output of tracing to stdout\n    // and log to a file included in the debug zip.\n    let stdout_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n        .with_target(false)\n        .without_time();\n\n    if let Ok(file) = create_tracing_log_file(base_path.clone()) {\n        let file_layer = tracing_subscriber::fmt::layer()\n            .with_writer(file)\n            .with_ansi(false);\n\n        let _ = tracing_subscriber::registry()\n            .with(EnvFilter::new(ENV_FILTER))\n            .with(stdout_layer)\n            .with(file_layer)\n            .try_init();\n    } else {\n        let _ = tracing_subscriber::registry()\n            .with(EnvFilter::new(ENV_FILTER))\n            .with(stdout_layer)\n            .try_init();\n    }\n\n    let initialize_then_run = async move {\n        // Preprocess args into contexts\n        let context = initialize_context(args, base_path).await?;\n        run(context).await\n    };\n\n    if let Err(err) = initialize_then_run.await {\n        error!(\n            \"mz-debug: fatal: {}\\nbacktrace: {}\",\n            err.display_with_causes(),\n            err.backtrace()\n        );\n        process::exit(1);\n    }\n}\n\nfn create_mz_connection_url",

        "contents": "pub fn install_enhanced_handler() {\n    panic::set_hook(Box::new(move |panic_info| {\n        // If we're catching an unwind, do nothing to let the unwind handler\n        // run.\n        let catching_unwind = CATCHING_UNWIND.with(|v| *v.borrow());\n        #[cfg(feature = \"async\")]\n        let catching_unwind_async = CATCHING_UNWIND_ASYNC.try_with(|v| *v).unwrap_or(false);\n        #[cfg(not(feature = \"async\"))]\n        let catching_unwind_async = false;\n        if catching_unwind != 0 || catching_unwind_async {\n            return;\n        }\n\n        // Report the panic to Sentry.\n        // Note that we can't use &#96;sentry_panic::panic_handler&#96; because that requires the panic\n        // integration to be enabled.\n        sentry::Hub::with_active(|hub| {\n            let event = sentry_panic::PanicIntegration::new().event_from_panic_info(panic_info);\n            hub.capture_event(event);\n            if let Some(client) = hub.client() {\n                client.flush(None);\n            }\n        });\n\n        // can't use if cfg!() here because that will require chrono::Utc import\n        #[cfg(feature = \"chrono\")]\n        let timestamp = Utc::now().format(\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%.6fZ  \").to_string();\n        #[cfg(not(feature = \"chrono\"))]\n        let timestamp = String::new();\n\n        let thread = thread::current();\n        let thread_name = thread.name().unwrap_or(\"&lt;unnamed&gt;\");\n\n        let msg = match panic_info.payload().downcast_ref::&lt;&amp;'static str&gt;() {\n            Some(s) =&gt; *s,\n            None =&gt; match panic_info.payload().downcast_ref::&lt;String&gt;() {\n                Some(s) =&gt; &amp;s[..],\n                None =&gt; \"Box&lt;Any&gt;\",\n            },\n        };\n\n        let location = if let Some(loc) = panic_info.location() {\n            loc.to_string()\n        } else {\n            \"&lt;unknown&gt;\".to_string()\n        };\n\n        // We unconditionally collect and display a short backtrace, as there's\n        // no practical situation where producing a backtrace in a panic message\n        // is undesirable. Panics are always unexpected, and we don't want to\n        // miss our chance to give ourselves as much context as possible.",

        "contents": "    let runtime = Arc::new(\n        tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\n            .worker_threads(ncpus_useful)\n            .thread_stack_size(3 * 1024 * 1024) // 3 MiB\n            // The default thread name exceeds the Linux limit on thread name\n            // length, so pick something shorter.\n            .thread_name_fn(|| {\n                static ATOMIC_ID: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n                let id = ATOMIC_ID.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);\n                format!(\"tokio:work-{}\", id)\n            })\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()?,\n    );\n\n    // Configure tracing to log the service name when using the process\n    // orchestrator, which intermingles log output from multiple services. Other\n    // orchestrators separate log output from different services.\n    args.tracing.log_prefix = if matches!(args.orchestrator, OrchestratorKind::Process) {\n        Some(\"environmentd\".to_string())\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    let metrics_registry = MetricsRegistry::new();\n    let tracing_handle = runtime.block_on(args.tracing.configure_tracing(\n        StaticTracingConfig {\n            service_name: \"environmentd\",\n            build_info: BUILD_INFO,\n        },\n        metrics_registry.clone(),\n    ))?;\n    register_runtime_metrics(\"main\", runtime.metrics(), &amp;metrics_registry);\n\n    let span = tracing::info_span!(\"environmentd::run\").entered();\n\n    info!(\"startup: envd init: beginning\");\n    info!(\"startup: envd init: preamble beginning\");\n\n    let metrics = Metrics::register_into(&amp;metrics_registry, BUILD_INFO);\n\n    runtime.block_on(mz_alloc::register_metrics_into(&amp;metrics_registry));\n    runtime.block_on(mz_metrics::register_metrics_into(\n        &amp;metrics_registry,\n        mz_dyncfgs::all_dyncfgs(),\n    ));\n\n    // Initialize fail crate for failpoint support\n    let _failpoint_scenario = FailScenario::setup();\n\n    // Configure connections.\n    let tls = args.tls.into_config()?;\n    let frontegg = FronteggAuthenticator::from_args(args.frontegg, &amp;metrics_registry)?;\n    let listeners_config: ListenersConfig =",

Panic utilities.\n\nuse std::any::Any;\nuse std::backtrace::Backtrace;\nuse std::borrow::Cow;\nuse std::cell::RefCell;\nuse std::fs::File;\nuse std::io::{self, Write as _};\nuse std::os::fd::FromRawFd;\nuse std::panic::{self, UnwindSafe};\nuse std::process;\nuse std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};\nuse std::time::Duration;\nuse std::{env, thread};\n\n#[cfg(feature = \"chrono\")]\nuse chrono::Utc;\nuse itertools::Itertools;\n#[cfg(feature = \"async\")]\nuse tokio::task_local;\n\nuse crate::iter::IteratorExt;\n\nthread_local! {\n    /// Keeps track of how many &#96;catch_unwind&#96; calls we are inside.\n    static CATCHING_UNWIND: RefCell&lt;usize&gt; = const { RefCell::new(0) };\n}\n\n#[cfg(feature = \"async\")]\ntask_local! {\n    pub(crate) static CATCHING_UNWIND_ASYNC: bool;\n}\n\n/// Overwrites the default panic handler with an enhanced panic handler.\n///\n/// The enhanced panic handler:\n///\n///   * Always emits a backtrace, regardless of how &#96;RUST_BACKTRACE&#96; was\n///     configured.\n///\n///   * Writes to stderr as atomically as possible, to minimize interleaving\n///     with concurrent log messages.\n///\n///   * Reports panics to Sentry.\n///\n///     Sentry installs its own panic hook by default that reports the panic and\n///     then forwards it to the previous panic hook. We can't use that hook\n///     because it would also report panics that we catch-unwind afterwards.\n///     Instead we are invoking the Sentry integration manually here, after the",

        "contents": " {\n    Kubernetes,\n    Process,\n}\n\n// TODO [Alex Hunt] move this to a shared function that can be imported by the\n// region-controller.\nfn aws_secrets_controller_prefix(env_id: &amp;EnvironmentId) -&gt; String {\n    format!(\"/user-managed/{}/\", env_id)\n}\nfn aws_secrets_controller_key_alias(env_id: &amp;EnvironmentId) -&gt; String {\n    // TODO [Alex Hunt] move this to a shared function that can be imported by the\n    // region-controller.\n    format!(\"alias/customer_key_{}\", env_id)\n}\n\npub fn main() {\n    let args = cli::parse_args(CliConfig {\n        env_prefix: Some(\"MZ_\"),\n        enable_version_flag: true,\n    });\n    if let Err(err) = run(args) {\n        panic!(\"environmentd: fatal: {}\", err.display_with_causes());\n    }\n}\n\nfn run(mut args: Args) -&gt; Result&lt;(), anyhow::Error&gt; {\n    mz_ore::panic::install_enhanced_handler();\n    let envd_start = Instant::now();\n\n    // Configure signal handling as soon as possible. We want signals to be\n    // handled to our liking ASAP.\n    sys::enable_sigusr2_coverage_dump()?;\n    sys::enable_termination_signal_cleanup()?;\n\n    let license_key = if let Some(license_key_file) = args.license_key",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/ore/src/tracing.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 321,
            "column": 30
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 365,
            "column": 20
          }
        },
        "contents": "pub async fn configure&lt;F&gt;(config: TracingConfig&lt;F&gt;) -&gt; Result&lt;TracingHandle, anyhow::Error&gt;\nwhere\n    F: Fn(&amp;tracing::Metadata&lt;'_&gt;) -&gt; sentry_tracing::EventFilter + Send + Sync + 'static,\n{\n    let stderr_log_layer: Box&lt;dyn Layer&lt;Registry&gt; + Send + Sync&gt; = match config.stderr_log.format {\n        StderrLogFormat::Text { prefix } =&gt; {\n            // See: https://no-color.org/\n            let no_color = std::env::var_os(\"NO_COLOR\").unwrap_or_else(|| \"\".into()) != \"\";\n            Box::new(\n                fmt::layer()\n                    .with_writer(io::stderr)\n                    .event_format(PrefixFormat {\n                        inner: format(),\n                        prefix,\n                    })\n                    .with_ansi(!no_color &amp;&amp; io::stderr().is_terminal()),\n            )\n        }\n        StderrLogFormat::Json =&gt; Box::new(\n            fmt::layer()\n                .with_writer(io::stderr)\n                .json()\n                .with_current_span(true),\n        ),\n    };\n    let (stderr_log_filter, stderr_log_filter_reloader) = reload::Layer::new({\n        let mut filter = config.stderr_log.filter;\n        for directive in LOGGING_DEFAULTS.iter() {\n            filter = filter.add_directive(directive.clone());\n        }\n        filter\n    });\n    // Add rate limiting for OpenTelemetry internal logs to prevent log spam\n    // when there are issues with the OpenTelemetry pipeline (e.g., channel full,\n    // connection errors). This only affects logs from \"opentelemetry*\" targets.\n    let otel_rate_limit_filter = OpenTelemetryRateLimitingFilter::new(Duration::from_secs(\n        OPENTELEMETRY_RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF_SECS,\n    ));\n    // IMPORTANT: The order matters here. The outer filter's &#96;max_level_hint()&#96; is used\n    // to determine the global tracing level. The &#96;otel_rate_limit_filter&#96; doesn't provide\n    // a &#96;max_level_hint()&#96; (defaults to TRACE), so the &#96;stderr_log_filter&#96; (EnvFilter)\n    // must be the outer filter to ensure the correct max level is reported.\n    let stderr_log_layer = stderr_log_layer\n        .with_filter",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "pub async fn configure&lt;F&gt;(config: TracingConfig&lt;F&gt;) -&gt; Result&lt;TracingHandle, anyhow::Error&gt;",

        "contents": "                // we will continue to place this here, as the sentry layer only cares about\n                // events &lt;= INFO, so we want to use the fast-path if no other layer\n                // is interested in high-fidelity events.\n                .with_filter(filter);\n            let reloader = Arc::new(move |defaults: Vec&lt;Directive&gt;| {\n                // Please see the comment on &#96;with_filter&#96; above.\n                let mut filter = EnvFilter::new(\"info\");\n                // Re-apply our defaults on reload.\n                for directive in &amp;defaults {\n                    filter = filter.add_directive(directive.clone());\n                }\n                Ok(filter_handle.reload(filter)?)\n            });\n            (Some(layer), reloader)\n        } else {\n            let reloader = Arc::new(|_| Ok(()));\n            (None, reloader)\n        };\n\n    #[cfg(feature = \"capture\")]\n    let capture = config.capture.map(|storage| CaptureLayer::new(&amp;storage));\n\n    let stack = tracing_subscriber::registry();\n    let stack = stack.with(stderr_log_layer);\n    #[cfg(feature = \"capture\")]\n    let stack = stack.with(capture);\n    let stack = stack.with(otel_layer);\n    #[cfg(feature = \"tokio-console\")]\n    let stack = stack.with(tokio_console_layer);\n    let stack = stack.with(sentry_layer);\n\n    // Set the stack as a global subscriber.\n    assert!(GLOBAL_SUBSCRIBER.set(Arc::new(stack)).is_ok());\n    // Initialize the subscriber.\n    Arc::clone(GLOBAL_SUBSCRIBER.get().unwrap()).init();\n\n    #[cfg(feature = \"tokio-console\")]\n    if let Some(console_config) = config.tokio_console {\n        let endpoint = match console_config.listen_addr {\n            SocketAddr::Inet(addr) =&gt; format!(\"http://{addr}\"),\n            SocketAddr::Unix(addr) =&gt; format!(\"file://localhost{addr}\"),\n            SocketAddr::Turmoil(_) =&gt; unimplemented!(),\n        };\n        tracing::info!(\"starting tokio console on {endpoint}\");\n    }\n\n    let handle = TracingHandle {\n        stderr_log: stderr_log_reloader,",

        "contents": "    let (sentry_layer, sentry_reloader): (_, DirectiveReloader) =\n        if let Some(sentry_config) = config.sentry {\n            let guard = sentry::init((\n                sentry_config.dsn,\n                sentry::ClientOptions {\n                    attach_stacktrace: true,\n                    release: Some(format!(\"materialize@{0}\", config.build_version).into()),\n                    environment: sentry_config.environment.map(Into::into),\n                    ..Default::default()\n                },\n            ));\n\n            // Forgetting the guard ensures that the Sentry transport won't shut down for the\n            // lifetime of the process.\n            std::mem::forget(guard);\n\n            sentry::configure_scope(|scope| {\n                scope.set_tag(\"service_name\", config.service_name);\n                scope.set_tag(\"build_sha\", config.build_sha.to_string());\n                for (k, v) in sentry_config.tags {\n                    scope.set_tag(&amp;k, v);\n                }\n            });\n\n            let (filter, filter_handle) = reload::Layer::new({\n                // Please see the comment on &#96;with_filter&#96; below.\n                let mut filter = EnvFilter::new(\"info\");\n                for directive in SENTRY_DEFAULTS.iter() {\n                    filter = filter.add_directive(directive.clone());\n                }\n                filter\n            });\n            let layer = sentry_tracing::layer()\n                .event_filter",

        "contents": "use tokio_postgres::error::SqlState;\nuse tokio_stream::wrappers::UnboundedReceiverStream;\nuse tower_sessions::Session as TowerSession;\nuse tracing::{debug, info};\nuse tungstenite::protocol::frame::coding::CloseCode;\n\nuse crate::http::prometheus::PrometheusSqlQuery;\nuse crate::http::{\n    AuthError, AuthedClient, AuthedUser, MAX_REQUEST_SIZE, SESSION_DURATION, TowerSessionData,\n    WsState, init_ws,\n};\n\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum Error {\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Adapter(#[from] AdapterError),\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Axum(#[from] axum::Error),\n    #[error(\"SUBSCRIBE only supported over websocket\")]\n    SubscribeOnlyOverWs,\n    #[error(\"current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until end of transaction block\")]\n    AbortedTransaction,\n    #[error(\"unsupported via this API: {0}\")]\n    Unsupported(String),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    Unstructured(anyhow::Error),\n}\n\nimpl Error {\n    pub fn detail(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Error::Adapter(err) =&gt; err.detail(),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn hint(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Error::Adapter(err) =&gt; err.hint(),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn position(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;usize&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Error::Adapter(err) =&gt; err.position(),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn code(&amp;self) -&gt; SqlState {\n        match self {\n            Error::Adapter(err) =&gt; err.code(),\n            Error::AbortedTransaction =&gt; SqlState::IN_FAILED_SQL_TRANSACTION,\n            _ =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nstatic PER_REPLICA_LABELS: &amp;[&amp;str] = &amp;[\"replica_full_name\", \"instance_id\", \"replica_id\"];\n\nasync fn execute_promsql_query",

        "contents": "impl ErrorLogger {\n    pub fn new(dataflow_name: String) -&gt; Self {\n        Self { dataflow_name }\n    }\n\n    /// Log the given error.\n    ///\n    /// The logging format is optimized for surfacing errors with Sentry:\n    ///  * &#96;error&#96; is logged at ERROR level and will appear as the error title in Sentry.\n    ///    We require it to be a static string, to ensure that Sentry always merges instances of\n    ///    the same error together.\n    ///  * &#96;details&#96; is logged at WARN level and will appear in the breadcrumbs.\n    ///    Put relevant dynamic information here.\n    ///\n    /// The message that's logged at WARN level has the format\n    ///   \"[customer-data] {message} ({details})\"\n    /// We include the [customer-data] tag out of the expectation that &#96;details&#96; will always\n    /// contain some sensitive customer data. We include the &#96;message&#96; to make it possible to match\n    /// the breadcrumbs to their associated error in Sentry.\n    ///\n    // TODO(database-issues#5362): Rethink or justify our error logging strategy.\n    pub fn log(&amp;self, message: &amp;'static str, details: &amp;str) {\n        tracing::warn!(\n            dataflow = self.dataflow_name,\n            \"[customer-data] {message} ({details})\"\n        );\n        tracing::error!(message);\n    }\n\n    /// Like [&#96;Self::log&#96;], but panics in debug mode.\n    ///\n    /// Use this method to notify about errors that are certainly caused by bugs in Materialize.\n    pub fn soft_panic_or_log(&amp;self, message: &amp;'static str, details: &amp;str) {\n        tracing::warn!(\n            dataflow = self.dataflow_name,\n            \"[customer-data] {message} ({details})\"\n        );\n        mz_ore::soft_panic_or_log!(\"{}\", message);\n    }\n}",

limits,\n        Err(e) =&gt; {\n            trace!(\"unable to read adjusted nofile rlimit: {}\", e);\n            return;\n        }\n    };\n    trace!(\"adjusted nofile rlimit: ({}, {})\", soft, hard);\n\n    const RECOMMENDED_SOFT: u64 = 1024;\n    if soft &lt; RECOMMENDED_SOFT {\n        warn!(\n            \"soft nofile rlimit ({}) is dangerously low; at least {} is recommended\",\n            soft, RECOMMENDED_SOFT\n        )\n    }\n}\n\npub fn enable_sigusr2_coverage_dump() -&gt; Result&lt;(), anyhow::Error&gt; {\n    let action = signal::SigAction::new(\n        signal::SigHandler::Handler(handle_sigusr2_signal),\n        signal::SaFlags::SA_NODEFER | signal::SaFlags::SA_ONSTACK,\n        signal::SigSet::empty(),\n    );\n\n    unsafe { signal::sigaction(signal::SIGUSR2, &amp;action) }\n        .context(\"failed to install SIGUSR2 handler\")?;\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\npub fn enable_termination_signal_cleanup() -&gt; Result&lt;(), anyhow::Error&gt; {\n    let action = signal::SigAction::new(\n        signal::SigHandler::Handler(handle_termination_signal),\n        signal::SaFlags::SA_NODEFER | signal::SaFlags::SA_ONSTACK,\n        signal::SigSet::empty(),\n    );\n\n    for signum in &amp;[\n        signal::SIGHUP,\n        signal::SIGINT,\n        signal::SIGALRM,\n        signal::SIGTERM,\n        signal::SIGUSR1,\n    ] {\n        unsafe { signal::sigaction(*signum, &amp;action) }\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to install handler for {}\", signum))?;\n    }\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\nunsafe extern \"C\" {\n    fn __llvm_profile_write_file() -&gt; Let me search for:
1. Where this specific error message is produced ("column 0 is not of expected type")
2. How errors in the adapter/sql path include or can include backtraces

        "contents": "            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl fmt::Display for AdapterError {\n    fn fmt(&amp;self, f: &amp;mut fmt::Formatter) -&gt; fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            AdapterError::AbsurdSubscribeBounds { as_of, up_to } =&gt; {\n                write!(\n                    f,\n                    \"subscription lower bound (&#96;AS OF&#96;) is greater than its upper bound (&#96;UP TO&#96;): \\\n                     {as_of} &gt; {up_to}\",\n                )\n            }\n            AdapterError::AmbiguousSystemColumnReference =&gt; {\n                write!(\n                    f,\n                    \"cannot use wildcard expansions or NATURAL JOINs in a view that depends on \\\n                    system objects\"\n                )\n            }\n            AdapterError::ChangedPlan(e) =&gt; write!(f, \"{}\", e),\n            AdapterError::Catalog(e) =&gt; e.fmt(f),\n            AdapterError::DuplicateCursor(name) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"cursor {} already exists\", name.quoted())\n            }\n            AdapterError::Eval(e) =&gt; e.fmt(f),\n            AdapterError::Explain(e) =&gt; e.fmt(f),\n            AdapterError::IdExhaustionError =&gt; f.write_str(\"ID allocator exhausted all valid IDs\"),\n            AdapterError::Internal(e) =&gt; write!(f, \"internal error: {}\", e),\n            AdapterError::IntrospectionDisabled { .. } =&gt; write!(\n                f,\n                \"cannot read log sources of replica with disabled introspection\"\n            ),\n            AdapterError::InvalidLogDependency { object_type, .. } =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"{object_type} objects cannot depend on log sources\")\n            }\n            AdapterError::InvalidClusterReplicaAz { az, expected: _ } =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"unknown cluster replica availability zone {az}\",)\n            }\n            AdapterError::InvalidSetIsolationLevel =&gt; write!(\n                f,\n                \"SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL must be called before any query\"\n            ),\n            AdapterError::InvalidSetCluster =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"SET cluster cannot be called in an active transaction\")",

        "contents": "pub enum AdapterError {\n    /// A &#96;SUBSCRIBE&#96; was requested whose &#96;UP TO&#96; bound precedes its &#96;as_of&#96; timestamp\n    AbsurdSubscribeBounds {\n        as_of: mz_repr::Timestamp,\n        up_to: mz_repr::Timestamp,\n    },\n    /// Attempted to use a potentially ambiguous column reference expression with a system table.\n    // We don't allow this until https://github.com/MaterializeInc/database-issues/issues/4824 is\n    // resolved because it prevents us from adding columns to system tables.\n    AmbiguousSystemColumnReference,\n    /// An error occurred in a catalog operation.\n    Catalog(mz_catalog::memory::error::Error),\n    /// 1. The cached plan or descriptor changed,\n    /// 2. or some dependency of a statement disappeared during sequencing.\n    /// TODO(ggevay): we should refactor 2. usages to use &#96;ConcurrentDependencyDrop&#96; instead\n    /// (e.g., in MV sequencing)\n    ChangedPlan(String),\n    /// The cursor already exists.\n    DuplicateCursor(String),\n    /// An error while evaluating an expression.\n    Eval(EvalError),\n    /// An error occurred while planning the statement.\n    Explain(ExplainError),\n    /// The ID allocator exhausted all valid IDs.\n    IdExhaustionError,\n    /// Unexpected internal state was encountered.\n    Internal(String),\n    /// Attempted to read from log sources of a replica with disabled introspection.\n    IntrospectionDisabled {\n        log_names: Vec&lt;String&gt;,\n    },\n    /// Attempted to create an object dependent on log sources that doesn't support\n    /// log dependencies.\n    InvalidLogDependency {\n        object_type: String,\n        log_names: Vec&lt;String&gt;,\n    },\n    /// No such cluster replica size has been configured.\n    InvalidClusterReplicaAz {\n        az: String,\n        expected: Vec&lt;String&gt;,\n    },\n    /// SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL was called in the middle of a transaction.\n    InvalidSetIsolationLevel,\n    /// SET cluster was called in the middle of a transaction.\n    InvalidSetCluster,\n    /// No such storage instance size has been configured.",

        "contents": "}\n\nimpl From&lt;ExplainError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: ExplainError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        match e {\n            ExplainError::RecursionLimitError(e) =&gt; AdapterError::RecursionLimit(e),\n            e =&gt; AdapterError::Explain(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;mz_sql::catalog::CatalogError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: mz_sql::catalog::CatalogError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Catalog(mz_catalog::memory::error::Error::from(e))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;PlanError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: PlanError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        match e {\n            PlanError::UnknownCursor(name) =&gt; AdapterError::UnknownCursor(name),\n            _ =&gt; AdapterError::PlanError(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;OptimizerError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: OptimizerError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        use OptimizerError::*;\n        match e {\n            PlanError(e) =&gt; Self::PlanError(e),\n            RecursionLimitError(e) =&gt; Self::RecursionLimit(e),\n            EvalError(e) =&gt; Self::Eval(e),\n            InternalUnsafeMfpPlan(e) =&gt; Self::Internal(e),\n            Internal(e) =&gt; Self::Internal(e),\n            e =&gt; Self::Optimizer(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;NotNullViolation&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: NotNullViolation) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::ConstraintViolation(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;RecursionLimitError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: RecursionLimitError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::RecursionLimit(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;oneshot::error::RecvError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: oneshot::error::RecvError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Unstructured(e.into())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;StorageError&lt;mz_repr::Timestamp&gt;&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: StorageError&lt;mz_repr::Timestamp&gt;) -&gt; Self {\n        AdapterError::Storage(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;compute_error::InstanceExists&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: compute_error::InstanceExists) -&gt; Self {\n        AdapterError::Compute(e.into())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;TimestampError&gt; for AdapterError",

        "contents": "impl AdapterError {\n    pub fn into_response(self, severity: Severity) -&gt; ErrorResponse {\n        ErrorResponse {\n            severity,\n            code: self.code(),\n            message: self.to_string(),\n            detail: self.detail(),\n            hint: self.hint(),\n            position: self.position(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn position(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;usize&gt; {\n        match self {\n            AdapterError::ParseError(err) =&gt; Some(err.error.pos),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Reports additional details about the error, if any are available.\n    pub fn detail(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            AdapterError::AmbiguousSystemColumnReference =&gt; {\n                Some(\"This is a current limitation in Materialize\".into())\n            },\n            AdapterError::Catalog(c) =&gt; c.detail(),\n            AdapterError::Eval(e) =&gt; e.detail(),\n            AdapterError::RelationOutsideTimeDomain { relations, names } =&gt; Some(format!(\n                \"The following relations in the query are outside the transaction's time domain:\\n{}\\n{}\",\n                relations\n                    .iter()\n                    .map(|r| r.quoted().to_string())\n                    .collect::&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;&gt;()\n                    .join(\"\\n\"),\n                match names.is_empty() {\n                    true =&gt; \"No relations are available.\".to_string(),\n                    false =&gt; format!(\n                        \"Only the following relations are available:\\n{}\",\n                        names\n                            .iter()\n                            .map(|name| name.quoted().to_string())\n                            .collect::&lt;Vec&lt;_&gt;&gt;()\n                            .join(\"\\n\")\n                    ),\n                }\n            )),\n            AdapterError::SourceOrSinkSizeRequired { .. } =&gt; Some(\n                \"Either specify the cluster that will maintain this object via IN CLUSTER or ",

        "contents": "impl From&lt;TimestampError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: TimestampError) -&gt; Self {\n        let e: EvalError = e.into();\n        e.into()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;mz_sql_parser::parser::ParserStatementError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: mz_sql_parser::parser::ParserStatementError) -&gt; Self {\n        AdapterError::ParseError(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;VarError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: VarError) -&gt; Self {\n        let e: mz_catalog::memory::error::Error = e.into();\n        e.into()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;rbac::UnauthorizedError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: rbac::UnauthorizedError) -&gt; Self {\n        AdapterError::Unauthorized(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;mz_sql_parser::ast::IdentError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(value: mz_sql_parser::ast::IdentError) -&gt; Self {\n        AdapterError::PlanError(PlanError::InvalidIdent(value))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;mz_pgwire_common::ConnectionError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(value: mz_pgwire_common::ConnectionError) -&gt; Self {\n        match value {\n            mz_pgwire_common::ConnectionError::TooManyConnections { current, limit } =&gt; {\n                AdapterError::ResourceExhaustion {\n                    resource_type: \"connection\".into(),\n                    limit_name: \"max_connections\".into(),\n                    desired: (current + 1).to_string(),\n                    limit: limit.to_string(),\n                    current: current.to_string(),\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;NetworkPolicyError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(value: NetworkPolicyError) -&gt; Self {\n        AdapterError::NetworkPolicyDenied(value)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;ConnectionValidationError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: ConnectionValidationError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::ConnectionValidation(e)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Error for AdapterError {}",

      "codeBlock": {

        "contents": "impl&lt;T, E&gt; ResultExt&lt;T&gt; for Result&lt;T, E&gt;\nwhere\n    E: ShouldTerminateGracefully + Debug,\n{\n    fn unwrap_or_terminate(self, context: &amp;str) -&gt; T {\n        match self {\n            Ok(t) =&gt; t,\n            Err(e) if e.should_terminate_gracefully() =&gt; exit!(0, \"{context}: {e:?}\"),\n            Err(e) =&gt; panic!(\"{context}: {e:?}\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn maybe_terminate(self, context: &amp;str) -&gt; Self {\n        if let Err(e) = &amp;self {\n            if e.should_terminate_gracefully() {\n                exit!(0, \"{context}: {e:?}\");\n            }\n        }\n\n        self\n    }\n}\n\n/// A trait for errors that should terminate gracefully rather than panic\n/// the process.\ntrait ShouldTerminateGracefully {\n    /// Reports whether the error should terminate the process gracefully\n    /// rather than panic.\n    fn should_terminate_gracefully(&amp;self) -&gt; bool;\n}\n\nimpl ShouldTerminateGracefully for AdapterError {\n    fn should_terminate_gracefully(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match self {\n            AdapterError::Catalog(e) =&gt; e.should_terminate_gracefully(),\n            _ =&gt; false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ShouldTerminateGracefully for mz_catalog::memory::error::Error {\n    fn should_terminate_gracefully(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match &amp;self.kind {\n            mz_catalog::memory::error::ErrorKind::Durable(e) =&gt; e.should_terminate_gracefully(),\n            _ =&gt; false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ShouldTerminateGracefully for mz_catalog::durable::CatalogError {\n    fn should_terminate_gracefully(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        match &amp;self {\n            Self::Durable(e) =&gt; e.should_terminate_gracefully(),\n            _ =&gt; false,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl ShouldTerminateGracefully for DurableCatalogError",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/error.rs",
        "range": {
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 1059,
            "column": 13
          },
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 1125,
            "column": 40
          }
        },
        "contents": "            AdapterError::UnavailableFeature { feature, docs } =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"{} is not supported in this environment.\", feature)?;\n                if let Some(docs) = docs {\n                    write!(\n                        f,\n                        \" For more information consult the documentation at {docs}\"\n                    )?;\n                }\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            AdapterError::AlterClusterWhilePendingReplicas =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"cannot alter clusters with pending updates\")\n            }\n            AdapterError::ReplacementSchemaMismatch(_) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"replacement schema differs from target schema\")\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;anyhow::Error&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: anyhow::Error) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        match e.downcast::&lt;PlanError&gt;() {\n            Ok(plan_error) =&gt; AdapterError::PlanError(plan_error),\n            Err(e) =&gt; AdapterError::Unstructured(e),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;TryFromIntError&gt; for AdapterError {\n    fn from(e: TryFromIntError) -&gt; AdapterError {\n        AdapterError::Unstructured(e.into())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;TryFromDecimalError&gt;         "contents": ".into(),\n            ),\n            AdapterError::DDLTransactionRace =&gt; Some(\n                \"Currently, DDL transactions fail when any other DDL happens concurrently, \\\n                 even on unrelated schemas/clusters.\".into()\n            ),\n            AdapterError::CollectionUnreadable { .. } =&gt; Some(\n                \"This could be because the collection has recently been dropped.\".into()\n            ),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn code(&amp;self) -&gt; SqlState {\n        // TODO(benesch): we should only use &#96;SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR&#96; for\n        // those errors that are truly internal errors. At the moment we have\n        // a various classes of uncategorized errors that use this error code\n        // inappropriately.\n        match self {\n            // DATA_EXCEPTION to match what Postgres returns for degenerate\n            // range bounds\n            AdapterError::AbsurdSubscribeBounds { .. } =&gt; SqlState::DATA_EXCEPTION,\n            AdapterError::AmbiguousSystemColumnReference =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n            AdapterError::Catalog(e) =&gt; match &amp;e.kind {\n                mz_catalog::memory::error::ErrorKind::VarError(e) =&gt; match e {\n                    VarError::ConstrainedParameter { .. } =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE,\n                    VarError::FixedValueParameter { .. } =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE,\n                    VarError::InvalidParameterType { .. } =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE,\n                    VarError::InvalidParameterValue { .. } =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE,\n                    VarError::ReadOnlyParameter(_) =&gt; SqlState::CANT_CHANGE_RUNTIME_PARAM,\n                    VarError::UnknownParameter(_) =&gt; SqlState::UNDEFINED_OBJECT,\n                    VarError::RequiresUnsafeMode { .. } =&gt; SqlState::CANT_CHANGE_RUNTIME_PARAM,\n                    VarError::RequiresFeatureFlag { .. } =&gt; SqlState::CANT_CHANGE_RUNTIME_PARAM,\n                },\n                _ =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n            },\n            AdapterError::ChangedPlan(_) =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n            AdapterError::DuplicateCursor(_) =&gt; SqlState::DUPLICATE_CURSOR,",

      "codeBlock": {
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "./src/adapter/src/optimize.rs",

            "line": 322,

        },
        "contents": "#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum OptimizerError {\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    PlanError(#[from] PlanError),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    RecursionLimitError(#[from] RecursionLimitError),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    TransformError(#[from] TransformError),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    EvalError(#[from] EvalError),\n    #[error(\"cannot materialize call to {0}\")]\n    UnmaterializableFunction(UnmaterializableFunc),\n    #[error(\"cannot call {func} in {context} \")]\n    UncallableFunction {\n        func: UnmaterializableFunc,\n        context: &amp;'static str,\n    },\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    UnsupportedTemporalExpression(String),\n    /// This is a specific kind of internal error. It's distinct from &#96;Internal&#96;, because we want to\n    /// catch it and swallow it in some cases.\n    #[error(\"internal optimizer error: MfpPlan couldn't be converted into SafeMfpPlan\")]\n    InternalUnsafeMfpPlan(String),\n    #[error(\"internal optimizer error: {0}\")]\n    Internal(String),\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;String&gt; for OptimizerError {\n    fn from(msg: String) -&gt; Self {\n        Self::Internal(msg)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl OptimizerError {\n    pub fn detail(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Self::UnmaterializableFunction(UnmaterializableFunc::CurrentTimestamp) =&gt; {\n                Some(\"See: https://materialize.com/docs/sql/functions/now_and_mz_now/\".into())\n            }\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn hint(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Self::UnmaterializableFunction(UnmaterializableFunc::CurrentTimestamp) =&gt; {\n                Some(\"In temporal filters &#96;mz_now()&#96; may work instead.\".into())\n            }\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;TimestampError&gt; for OptimizerError {\n    fn from(value: TimestampError) -&gt; Self {\n        OptimizerError::EvalError(EvalError::from(value))\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;anyhow::Error&gt; for OptimizerError {\n    fn from(value: anyhow::Error) -&gt; Self {\n        OptimizerError::Internal(value.to_string())\n    }\n}\n\nimpl MaybeShouldPanic for OptimizerError",
        "signatures": {},
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "text": "#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]",
            "lineNumber": 258
          },
          {
            "text": "pub enum OptimizerError {",
            "lineNumber": 259
          },
          {
            "text": "    #[error(\"{0}\")]",
            "lineNumber": 260
          },
          {
            "text": "    PlanError(#[from] PlanError),",
            "lineNumber": 261

{\n                write!(\n                    f,\n                    \"creating {resource_type} would violate {limit_name} limit (desired: {desired}, limit: {limit}, current: {current})\"\n                )\n            }\n            AdapterError::ResultSize(e) =&gt; write!(f, \"{e}\"),\n            AdapterError::SafeModeViolation(feature) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"cannot create {} in safe mode\", feature)\n            }\n            AdapterError::SubscribeOnlyTransaction =&gt; {\n                f.write_str(\"SUBSCRIBE in transactions must be the only read statement\")\n            }\n            AdapterError::Optimizer(e) =&gt; e.fmt(f),\n            AdapterError::UnallowedOnCluster {\n                depends_on,\n                cluster,\n            } =&gt; {\n                let items = depends_on.into_iter().map(|item| item.quoted()).join(\", \");\n                write!(\n                    f,\n                    \"querying the following items {items} is not allowed from the {} cluster\",\n                    cluster.quoted()\n                )\n            }\n            AdapterError::Unauthorized(unauthorized) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"{unauthorized}\")\n            }\n            AdapterError::UnknownCursor(name) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"cursor {} does not exist\", name.quoted())\n            }\n            AdapterError::UnknownLoginRole(name) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"role {} does not exist\", name.quoted())\n            }\n            AdapterError::Unsupported(features) =&gt; write!(f, \"{} are not supported\", features),\n            AdapterError::Unstructured(e) =&gt; write!(f, \"{}\", e.display_with_causes()),\n            AdapterError::WriteOnlyTransaction =&gt; f.write_str(\"transaction in write-only mode\"),\n            AdapterError::UnknownPreparedStatement(name) =&gt; {\n                write!(f, \"prepared statement {} does not exist\", name.quoted())",

Please contact support.\".to_string()),\n            AdapterError::NetworkPolicyDenied(reason)=&gt; Some(format!(\"{reason}.\")),\n            AdapterError::ReplacementSchemaMismatch(diff) =&gt; {\n                let mut lines: Vec&lt;_&gt; = diff.column_diffs.iter().map(|(idx, diff)| {\n                    let pos = idx + 1;\n                    match diff {\n                        ColumnDiff::Missing { name } =&gt; {\n                            let name = name.as_str().quoted();\n                            format!(\"missing column {name} at position {pos}\")\n                        }\n                        ColumnDiff::Extra { name } =&gt; {\n                            let name = name.as_str().quoted();\n                            format!(\"extra column {name} at position {pos}\")\n                        }\n                        ColumnDiff::TypeMismatch { name, left, right } =&gt; {\n                            let name = name.as_str().quoted();\n                            format!(\"column {name} at position {pos}: type mismatch (target: {left:?}, replacement: {right:?})\")\n                        }\n                        ColumnDiff::NullabilityMismatch { name, left, right } =&gt; {\n                            let name = name.as_str().quoted();\n                            let left = if *left { \"NULL\" } else { \"NOT NULL\" };\n                            let right = if *right { \"NULL\" } else { \"NOT NULL\" };\n                            format!(\"column {name} at position {pos}: nullability mismatch (target: {left}, replacement: {right})\")\n                        }\n                        ColumnDiff::NameMismatch { left, right } =&gt; {\n                            let left = left.as_str().quoted();",

        "contents": " =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n            },\n            AdapterError::UnallowedOnCluster { .. } =&gt; {\n                SqlState::S_R_E_PROHIBITED_SQL_STATEMENT_ATTEMPTED\n            }\n            AdapterError::Unauthorized(_) =&gt; SqlState::INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE,\n            AdapterError::UnknownCursor(_) =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_CURSOR_NAME,\n            AdapterError::UnknownPreparedStatement(_) =&gt; SqlState::UNDEFINED_PSTATEMENT,\n            AdapterError::UnknownLoginRole(_) =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_AUTHORIZATION_SPECIFICATION,\n            AdapterError::UnknownClusterReplica { .. } =&gt; SqlState::UNDEFINED_OBJECT,\n            AdapterError::UnrecognizedConfigurationParam(_) =&gt; SqlState::UNDEFINED_OBJECT,\n            AdapterError::Unsupported(..) =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n            AdapterError::UnavailableFeature { .. } =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n            AdapterError::Unstructured(_) =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n            AdapterError::UntargetedLogRead { .. } =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n            AdapterError::DDLTransactionRace =&gt; SqlState::T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE,\n            AdapterError::TransactionDryRun { .. } =&gt; SqlState::T_R_SERIALIZATION_FAILURE,\n            // It's not immediately clear which error code to use here because a\n            // \"write-only transaction\", \"single table write transaction\", or \"ddl only\n            // transaction\" are not things in Postgres. This error code is the generic \"bad txn\n            // thing\" code, so it's probably the best choice.\n            AdapterError::WriteOnlyTransaction =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_TRANSACTION_STATE,\n            AdapterError::DDLOnlyTransaction =&gt; SqlState::INVALID_TRANSACTION_STATE,\n            AdapterError::Storage(_) | AdapterError::Compute(_) | AdapterError::Orchestrator(_) =&gt; {\n                SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR\n            }\n            AdapterError::DependentObject(_) =&gt; SqlState::DEPENDENT_OBJECTS_STILL_EXIST,\n            AdapterError::InvalidAlter(_, _) =&gt; SqlState::FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED,\n            AdapterError::ConnectionValidation(_) =&gt; SqlState::SYSTEM_ERROR,\n            // &#96;DATA_EXCEPTION&#96;, similarly to &#96;AbsurdSubscribeBounds&#96;.",

          }
        },
        "contents": "        // ended.\n        if let Some(logging_id) = statement_logging_id {\n            let reason = match &amp;result {\n                // Streaming results are handled asynchronously by the coordinator\n                Ok(Some(ExecuteResponse::SendingRowsStreaming { .. })) =&gt; {\n                    // Don't log here - the peek is still executing.\n                    // It will be logged when handle_peek_notification is called.\n                    return result;\n                }\n                // COPY TO needs to check its inner response\n                Ok(Some(resp @ ExecuteResponse::CopyTo { resp: inner, .. })) =&gt; {\n                    match inner.as_ref() {\n                        ExecuteResponse::SendingRowsStreaming { .. } =&gt; {\n                            // Don't log here - the peek is still executing.\n                            // It will be logged when handle_peek_notification is called.\n                            return result;\n                        }\n                        // For non-streaming COPY TO responses, use the outer CopyTo for conversion\n                        _ =&gt; resp.into(),\n                    }\n                }\n                // Bailout case, which should not happen\n                Ok(None) =&gt; {\n                    soft_panic_or_log!(\n                        \"Bailed out from &#96;try_frontend_peek_inner&#96; after we already logged the beginning of statement execution.\"\n                    );\n                    // This statement will be handled by the old peek sequencing, which will do its\n                    // own statement logging from the beginning. So, let's close out this one.\n                    self.log_ended_execution(\n                        logging_id,\n                        StatementEndedExecutionReason::Errored {\n                            error: \"Internal error: bailed out from &#96;try_frontend_peek_inner&#96;\"\n                                .to_string(),\n                        },\n                    );\n                    return result;\n                }\n                // All other success responses - use the From implementation\n                // TODO(peek-seq): After we delete the old peek sequencing, we'll be able to adjust",

        "contents": "#[derive(thiserror::Error, Debug)]\npub enum AppendWebhookError {\n    // A secret that we need for validation has gone missing.\n    #[error(\"could not read a required secret\")]\n    MissingSecret,\n    #[error(\"the provided request body is not UTF-8: {msg}\")]\n    InvalidUtf8Body { msg: String },\n    #[error(\"the provided request body is not valid JSON: {msg}\")]\n    InvalidJsonBody { msg: String },\n    #[error(\"webhook source '{database}.{schema}.{name}' does not exist\")]\n    UnknownWebhook {\n        database: String,\n        schema: String,\n        name: String,\n    },\n    #[error(\"failed to validate the request\")]\n    ValidationFailed,\n    // Note: we should _NEVER_ add more detail to this error, including the actual error we got\n    // when running validation. This is because the error messages might contain info about the\n    // arguments provided to the validation expression, we could contains user SECRETs. So by\n    // including any more detail we might accidentally expose SECRETs.\n    #[error(\"validation error\")]\n    ValidationError,\n    #[error(\"internal channel closed\")]\n    ChannelClosed,\n    #[error(\"internal error: {0:?}\")]\n    InternalError(#[from] anyhow::Error),\n    #[error(\"internal storage failure! {0:?}\")]\n    StorageError(#[from] StorageError&lt;mz_repr::Timestamp&gt;),\n}\n\n/// Contains all of the components necessary for running webhook validation.\n///\n/// To actually validate a webhook request call [&#96;AppendWebhookValidator::eval&#96;].\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct AppendWebhookValidator {\n    validation: WebhookValidation,\n    secrets_reader: CachingSecretsReader,\n}\n\nimpl AppendWebhookValidator {\n    pub fn new",

        "contents": "use tokio_postgres::error::SqlState;\nuse tokio_stream::wrappers::UnboundedReceiverStream;\nuse tower_sessions::Session as TowerSession;\nuse tracing::{debug, info};\nuse tungstenite::protocol::frame::coding::CloseCode;\n\nuse crate::http::prometheus::PrometheusSqlQuery;\nuse crate::http::{\n    AuthError, AuthedClient, AuthedUser, MAX_REQUEST_SIZE, SESSION_DURATION, TowerSessionData,\n    WsState, init_ws,\n};\n\n#[derive(Debug, thiserror::Error)]\npub enum Error {\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Adapter(#[from] AdapterError),\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Json(#[from] serde_json::Error),\n    #[error(transparent)]\n    Axum(#[from] axum::Error),\n    #[error(\"SUBSCRIBE only supported over websocket\")]\n    SubscribeOnlyOverWs,\n    #[error(\"current transaction is aborted, commands ignored until end of transaction block\")]\n    AbortedTransaction,\n    #[error(\"unsupported via this API: {0}\")]\n    Unsupported(String),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    Unstructured(anyhow::Error),\n}\n\nimpl Error {\n    pub fn detail(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Error::Adapter(err) =&gt; err.detail(),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn hint(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;String&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Error::Adapter(err) =&gt; err.hint(),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn position(&amp;self) -&gt; Option&lt;usize&gt; {\n        match self {\n            Error::Adapter(err) =&gt; err.position(),\n            _ =&gt; None,\n        }\n    }\n\n    pub fn code(&amp;self) -&gt; SqlState {\n        match self {\n            Error::Adapter(err) =&gt; err.code(),\n            Error::AbortedTransaction =&gt; SqlState::IN_FAILED_SQL_TRANSACTION,\n            _ =&gt; SqlState::INTERNAL_ERROR,\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nstatic PER_REPLICA_LABELS: &amp;[&amp;str] = &amp;[\"replica_full_name\", \"instance_id\", \"replica_id\"];\n\nasync fn execute_promsql_query",

        "contents": "            }\n        };\n\n        let client = AuthedClient::new(\n            &amp;adapter_client,\n            user.clone(),\n            peer_addr,\n            active_connection_counter.clone(),\n            helm_chart_version,\n            |session| {\n                session\n                    .vars_mut()\n                    .set_default(WELCOME_MESSAGE.name(), VarInput::Flat(&amp;false.format()))\n                    .expect(\"known to exist\")\n            },\n            options,\n            SYSTEM_TIME.clone(),\n        )\n        .await\n        .map_err(|e| {\n            let status = match e {\n                AdapterError::UserSessionsDisallowed | AdapterError::NetworkPolicyDenied(_) =&gt; {\n                    StatusCode::FORBIDDEN\n                }\n                _ =&gt; StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n            };\n            (status, Json(SqlError::from(e))).into_response()\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(client)\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Error)]\nenum AuthError {\n    #[error(\"role dissallowed\")]\n    RoleDisallowed(String),\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    Frontegg(#[from] FronteggError),\n    #[error(\"missing authorization header\")]\n    MissingHttpAuthentication {\n        include_www_authenticate_header: bool,\n    },\n    #[error(\"{0}\")]\n    MismatchedUser(String),\n    #[error(\"session expired\")]\n    SessionExpired,\n    #[error(\"failed to update session\")]\n    FailedToUpdateSession,\n    #[error(\"invalid credentials\")]\n    InvalidCredentials,\n}\n\nimpl IntoResponse for AuthError",

      "codeBlock": {

        },
        "contents": "pub struct SqlError {\n    pub message: String,\n    pub code: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub detail: Option&lt;String&gt;,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub hint: Option&lt;String&gt;,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub position: Option&lt;usize&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;Error&gt; for SqlError {\n    fn from(err: Error) -&gt; Self {\n        SqlError {\n            message: err.to_string(),\n            code: err.code().code().to_string(),\n            detail: err.detail(),\n            hint: err.hint(),\n            position: err.position(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;AdapterError&gt; for SqlError {\n    fn from(value: AdapterError) -&gt; Self {\n        Error::from(value).into()\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Deserialize, Serialize)]\n#[serde(tag = \"type\", content = \"payload\")]\npub enum WebSocketResponse {\n    ReadyForQuery(String),\n    Notice(Notice),\n    Rows(Description),\n    Row(Vec&lt;serde_json::Value&gt;),\n    CommandStarting(CommandStarting),\n    CommandComplete(String),\n    Error(SqlError),\n    ParameterStatus(ParameterStatus),\n    BackendKeyData(BackendKeyData),\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Notice {\n    message: String,\n    code: String,\n    severity: String,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub detail: Option&lt;String&gt;,\n    #[serde(skip_serializing_if = \"Option::is_none\")]\n    pub hint: Option&lt;String&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl Notice {\n    pub fn message(&amp;self) -&gt; &amp;str {\n        &amp;self.message\n    }\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct Description {\n    pub columns: Vec&lt;Column&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl From&lt;&amp;RelationDesc&gt; for Description",

        "contents": "        if is_err.is_err() {\n            // Mirror StateMachine::error, which sometimes will clean up the\n            // transaction state instead of always leaving it in Failed.\n            let txn = client.session().transaction();\n            match txn {\n                // Error can be called from describe and parse and so might not be in an active\n                // transaction.\n                TransactionStatus::Default | TransactionStatus::Failed(_) =&gt; {}\n                // In Started (i.e., a single statement) and implicit transactions cleanup themselves.\n                TransactionStatus::Started(_) | TransactionStatus::InTransactionImplicit(_) =&gt; {\n                    if let Err(err) = client.end_transaction(EndTransactionAction::Rollback).await {\n                        let err = SqlResult::err(client, err);\n                        let _ = send_and_retire(err.into(), client, sender).await?;\n                    }\n                }\n                // Explicit transactions move to failed.\n                TransactionStatus::InTransaction(_) =&gt; {\n                    client.fail_transaction();\n                }\n            }\n            return Ok(Err(()));\n        }\n    }\n    Ok(Ok(()))\n}\n\n/// Executes an entire [&#96;SqlRequest&#96;].\n///\n/// See the user-facing documentation about the HTTP API for a description of\n/// the semantics of this function.\nasync fn execute_request&lt;S: ResultSender&gt;(\n    client: &amp;mut AuthedClient,\n    request: SqlRequest,\n    sender: &amp;mut S,\n)",

        "contents": "    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ResultSender for SqlResponse {\n    // The first component of the return value is\n    // Err if sending to the client\n    // produced an error and the server should disconnect. It is Ok(Err) if the statement\n    // produced an error and should error the transaction, but remain connected. It is Ok(Ok(()))\n    // if the statement succeeded.\n    // The second component of the return value is &#96;Some&#96; if execution still\n    // needs to be retired for statement logging purposes.\n    async fn add_result(\n        &amp;mut self,\n        _client: &amp;mut SessionClient,\n        res: StatementResult,\n    ) -&gt; (\n        Result&lt;Result&lt;(), ()&gt;, Error&gt;,\n        Option&lt;(StatementEndedExecutionReason, ExecuteContextGuard)&gt;,\n    ) {\n        let (res, stmt_logging) = match res {\n            StatementResult::SqlResult(res) =&gt; {\n                let is_err = matches!(res, SqlResult::Err { .. });\n                self.results.push(res);\n                let res = if is_err { Err(()) } else { Ok(()) };\n                (res, None)\n            }\n            StatementResult::Subscribe { ctx_extra, .. } =&gt; {\n                let message = \"SUBSCRIBE only supported over websocket\";\n                self.results.push(SqlResult::Err {\n                    error: Error::SubscribeOnlyOverWs.into(),\n                    notices: Vec::new(),\n                });\n                (\n                    Err(()),\n                    Some((\n                        StatementEndedExecutionReason::Errored {\n                            error: message.into(),\n                        },\n                        ctx_extra,\n                    )),\n                )\n            }\n        };\n        (Ok(res), stmt_logging)\n    }\n\n    fn connection_error(&amp;mut self) -&gt; BoxFuture&lt;'_, Error&gt; {\n        Box::pin(futures::future::pending())\n    }\n\n    fn allow_subscribe(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        false\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait]\nimpl ResultSender for WebSocket {\n    const SUPPORTS_STREAMING_NOTICES: bool = true;",

        "contents": "    for msg in msgs {\n        let _ = ws\n            .send(Message::Text(\n                serde_json::to_string(&amp;msg).expect(\"must serialize\").into(),\n            ))\n            .await;\n    }\n\n    // Send any notices that might have been generated on startup.\n    let notices = session.drain_notices();\n    if let Err(err) = forward_notices(&amp;mut ws, notices).await {\n        debug!(\"failed to forward notices to WebSocket, {err:?}\");\n        return;\n    }\n\n    loop {\n        // Handle timeouts first so we don't execute any statements when there's a pending timeout.\n        let msg = select! {\n            biased;\n\n            // &#96;recv_timeout()&#96; is cancel-safe as per it's docs.\n            Some(timeout) = client.client.recv_timeout() =&gt; {\n                client.client.terminate().await;\n                // We must wait for the client to send a request before we can send the error\n                // response. Although this isn't the PG wire protocol, we choose to mirror it by\n                // only sending errors as responses to requests.\n                let _ = ws.recv().await;\n                let err = Error::from(AdapterError::from(timeout));\n                let _ = send_ws_response(&amp;mut ws, WebSocketResponse::Error(err.into())).await;\n                return;\n            },\n            message = ws.recv() =&gt; message,\n        };\n\n        client.client.remove_idle_in_transaction_session_timeout();\n\n        let msg = match msg {\n            Some(Ok(msg)) =&gt; msg,\n            _ =&gt; {\n                // client disconnected\n                return;\n            }\n        };\n\n        let req: Result&lt;SqlRequest, Error&gt; =",

        "contents": "        }\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn handle_promsql(\n    mut client: AuthedClient,\n    queries: &amp;[PrometheusSqlQuery&lt;'_&gt;],\n) -&gt; MetricsRegistry {\n    let metrics_registry = MetricsRegistry::new();\n    let mut metrics_by_name = BTreeMap::new();\n\n    for query in queries {\n        handle_promsql_query(&amp;mut client, query, &amp;metrics_registry, &amp;mut metrics_by_name).await;\n    }\n\n    metrics_registry\n}\n\npub async fn handle_sql(\n    mut client: AuthedClient,\n    Json(request): Json&lt;SqlRequest&gt;,\n) -&gt; impl IntoResponse {\n    let mut res = SqlResponse {\n        results: Vec::new(),\n    };\n    // Don't need to worry about timeouts or resetting cancel here because there is always exactly 1\n    // request.\n    match execute_request(&amp;mut client, request, &amp;mut res).await {\n        Ok(()) =&gt; Ok(Json(res)),\n        Err(e) =&gt; Err((StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, e.to_string())),\n    }\n}\n\npub async fn handle_sql_ws(\n    State(state): State&lt;WsState&gt;,",

        "contents": "    // connected.\n    fn connection_error(&amp;mut self) -&gt; BoxFuture&lt;'_, Error&gt; {\n        Box::pin(async {\n            let mut tick = time::interval(Duration::from_secs(1));\n            tick.tick().await;\n            loop {\n                tick.tick().await;\n                if let Err(err) = self.send(Message::Ping(Vec::new().into())).await {\n                    return err.into();\n                }\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    fn allow_subscribe(&amp;self) -&gt; bool {\n        true\n    }\n\n    async fn emit_streaming_notices(&amp;mut self, notices: Vec&lt;AdapterNotice&gt;) -&gt; Result&lt;(), Error&gt; {\n        forward_notices(self, notices).await\n    }\n}\n\nasync fn await_rows&lt;S, F, R&gt;(sender: &amp;mut S, client: &amp;mut SessionClient, f: F) -&gt; Result&lt;R, Error&gt;\nwhere\n    S: ResultSender,\n    F: Future&lt;Output = R&gt; + Send,\n{\n    let mut f = pin!(f);\n    loop {\n        tokio::select! {\n            notice = client.session().recv_notice(), if S::SUPPORTS_STREAMING_NOTICES =&gt; {\n                sender.emit_streaming_notices(vec![notice]).await?;\n            }\n            e = sender.connection_error() =&gt; return Err(e),\n            r = &amp;mut f =&gt; return Ok(r),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\nasync fn send_and_retire&lt;S: ResultSender&gt;(\n    res: StatementResult,\n    client: &amp;mut SessionClient,\n    sender: &amp;mut S,\n) -&gt; Result&lt;Result&lt;(), ()&gt;, Error&gt; {\n    let (res, stmt_logging) = sender.add_result(client, res).await;\n    if let Some((reason, ctx_extra)) = stmt_logging {\n        client.retire_execute(ctx_extra, reason);\n    }\n    res\n}\n\n/// Returns Ok(Err) if any statement error'd during execution.\nasync fn execute_stmt_group&lt;S: ResultSender&gt;(\n    client: &amp;mut SessionClient,\n    sender: &amp;mut S,",

        "contents": "use crate::http::WebhookState;\n\npub async fn handle_webhook(\n    State(WebhookState {\n        adapter_client_rx,\n        webhook_cache,\n    }): State&lt;WebhookState&gt;,\n    Path((database, schema, name)): Path&lt;(String, String, String)&gt;,\n    headers: http::HeaderMap,\n    body: Bytes,\n) -&gt; impl IntoResponse {\n    let adapter_client = adapter_client_rx.clone().await.expect(\"sender not dropped\");\n    // Collect headers into a map, while converting them into strings.\n    let mut headers_s = BTreeMap::new();\n    for (name, val) in headers.iter() {\n        if let Ok(val_s) = val.to_str().map(|s| s.to_string()) {\n            // If a header is included more than once, bail returning an error to the user.\n            let existing = headers_s.insert(name.as_str().to_string(), val_s);\n            if existing.is_some() {\n                let msg = format!(\"{} provided more than once\", name.as_str());\n                return Err(WebhookError::InvalidHeaders(msg));\n            }\n        }\n    }\n    let headers = Arc::new(headers_s);\n\n    // Append to the webhook source, retrying if we race with a concurrent &#96;ALTER SOURCE&#96; op.\n    Retry::default()\n        .max_tries(2)\n        .retry_async(|_| async {\n            let result = append_webhook(\n                &amp;adapter_client,\n                &amp;webhook_cache,\n                &amp;database,\n                &amp;schema,\n                &amp;name,\n                &amp;body,\n                &amp;headers,\n            )\n            .await;\n\n            // Note: think carefully before adding more errors here, we need to make sure we don't\n            // append data more than once.\n            match result {\n                Ok(()) =&gt; RetryResult::Ok(()),\n                Err(e @ AppendWebhookError::ChannelClosed) =&gt; RetryResult::RetryableErr(e),\n                Err(e) =&gt; RetryResult::FatalErr(e),\n            }\n        })\n        .await?;\n\n    Ok::&lt;_, WebhookError&gt;(())\n}\n\n/// Append the provided &#96;body&#96; and &#96;headers&#96; to the webhook source identified via &#96;database&#96;,\n/// &#96;schema&#96;, and &#96;name&#96;.\nasync fn append_webhook(\n    adapter_client: &amp;mz_adapter::Client,\n    webhook_cache: &amp;WebhookAppenderCache",

        "contents": "pub async fn handle_sql_ws(\n    State(state): State&lt;WsState&gt;,\n    existing_user: Option&lt;Extension&lt;AuthedUser&gt;&gt;,\n    ws: WebSocketUpgrade,\n    ConnectInfo(addr): ConnectInfo&lt;SocketAddr&gt;,\n    tower_session: Option&lt;Extension&lt;TowerSession&gt;&gt;,\n) -&gt; impl IntoResponse {\n    // An upstream middleware may have already provided the user for us\n    let user = match (existing_user, tower_session) {\n        (Some(Extension(user)), _) =&gt; Some(user),\n        (None, Some(session)) =&gt; {\n            if let Ok(Some(session_data)) = session.get::&lt;TowerSessionData&gt;(\"data\").await {\n                // Check session expiration\n                if session_data\n                    .last_activity\n                    .elapsed()\n                    .unwrap_or(Duration::MAX)\n                    &gt; SESSION_DURATION\n                {\n                    let _ = session.delete().await;\n                    return Err(AuthError::SessionExpired);\n                }\n                // Update last activity\n                let mut updated_data = session_data.clone();\n                updated_data.last_activity = SystemTime::now();\n                session\n                    .insert(\"data\", &amp;updated_data)\n                    .await\n                    .map_err(|_| AuthError::FailedToUpdateSession)?;\n                // User is authenticated via session\n                Some(AuthedUser {\n                    name: session_data.username,\n                    external_metadata_rx: None,\n                    internal_metadata: Some(session_data.internal_metadata),\n                })\n            } else {\n                None\n            }\n        }\n        _ =&gt; None,\n    };\n\n    let addr = Box::new(addr.ip());\n    Ok(ws\n        .max_message_size(MAX_REQUEST_SIZE)\n        .on_upgrade(|ws| async move { run_ws(&amp;state, user, *addr, ws).await }))\n}\n\n#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]\n#[serde(untagged)]\npub enum WebSocketAuth {\n    Basic {\n        user: String,\n        password: Password",

        "contents": "impl IntoResponse for WebhookError {\n    fn into_response(self) -&gt; axum::response::Response {\n        match self {\n            e @ WebhookError::NotFound(_) | e @ WebhookError::SecretMissing =&gt; {\n                (StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, e.to_string()).into_response()\n            }\n            e @ WebhookError::InvalidBody { .. }\n            | e @ WebhookError::ValidationFailed\n            | e @ WebhookError::ValidationError =&gt; {\n                (StatusCode::BAD_REQUEST, e.to_string()).into_response()\n            }\n            e @ WebhookError::InvalidHeaders(_) =&gt; {\n                (StatusCode::UNAUTHORIZED, e.to_string()).into_response()\n            }\n            e @ WebhookError::Unavailable =&gt; {\n                (StatusCode::SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE, e.to_string()).into_response()\n            }\n            e @ WebhookError::InternalStorageError(StorageError::ResourceExhausted(_)) =&gt; {\n                (StatusCode::TOO_MANY_REQUESTS, e.to_string()).into_response()\n            }\n            e @ WebhookError::InternalStorageError(_) =&gt; {\n                (StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, e.to_string()).into_response()\n            }\n            WebhookError::Internal(e) =&gt; (\n                StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR,\n                e.root_cause().to_string(),\n            )\n                .into_response(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use std::collections::{BTreeMap, BTreeSet};",

        "contents": "        let (is_err, msgs, stmt_logging) = match res {\n            StatementResult::SqlResult(SqlResult::Rows {\n                tag,\n                rows,\n                desc,\n                notices,\n            }) =&gt; {\n                let mut msgs = vec![WebSocketResponse::Rows(desc)];\n                msgs.extend(rows.into_iter().map(WebSocketResponse::Row));\n                msgs.push(WebSocketResponse::CommandComplete(tag));\n                msgs.extend(notices.into_iter().map(WebSocketResponse::Notice));\n                (false, msgs, None)\n            }\n            StatementResult::SqlResult(SqlResult::Ok {\n                ok,\n                parameters,\n                notices,\n            }) =&gt; {\n                let mut msgs = vec![WebSocketResponse::CommandComplete(ok)];\n                msgs.extend(notices.into_iter().map(WebSocketResponse::Notice));\n                msgs.extend(\n                    parameters\n                        .into_iter()\n                        .map(WebSocketResponse::ParameterStatus),\n                );\n                (false, msgs, None)\n            }\n            StatementResult::SqlResult(SqlResult::Err { error, notices }) =&gt; {\n                let mut msgs = vec![WebSocketResponse::Error(error)];\n                msgs.extend(notices.into_iter().map(WebSocketResponse::Notice));\n                (true, msgs, None)\n            }\n            StatementResult::Subscribe {\n                ref desc,",

        "contents": " \"replica_id\"];\n\nasync fn execute_promsql_query(\n    client: &amp;mut AuthedClient,\n    query: &amp;PrometheusSqlQuery&lt;'_&gt;,\n    metrics_registry: &amp;MetricsRegistry,\n    metrics_by_name: &amp;mut BTreeMap&lt;String, GenericGaugeVec&lt;AtomicF64&gt;&gt;,\n    cluster: Option&lt;(&amp;Cluster, &amp;ClusterReplica)&gt;,\n) {\n    assert_eq!(query.per_replica, cluster.is_some());\n\n    let mut res = SqlResponse {\n        results: Vec::new(),\n    };\n\n    execute_request(client, query.to_sql_request(cluster), &amp;mut res)\n        .await\n        .expect(\"valid SQL query\");\n\n    let result = match res.results.as_slice() {\n        // Each query issued is preceded by several SET commands\n        // to make sure it is routed to the right cluster replica.\n        [\n            SqlResult::Ok { .. },\n            SqlResult::Ok { .. },\n            SqlResult::Ok { .. },\n            result,\n        ] =&gt; result,\n        // Transient errors are fine, like if the cluster or replica\n        // was dropped before the promsql query was executed. We\n        // should not see errors in the steady state.\n        _ =&gt; {\n            info!(\n                \"error executing prometheus query {}: {:?}\",\n                query.metric_name, res\n            );\n            return;\n        }\n    };\n\n    let SqlResult::Rows { desc, rows, .. } = result else {\n        info!(\n            \"did not receive rows for SQL query for prometheus metric {}: {:?}, {:?}\",\n            query.metric_name, result, cluster\n        );\n        return;\n    };\n\n    let gauge_vec = metrics_by_name\n        .entry",

        "contents": "    match request {\n        SqlRequest::Simple { query } =&gt; match parse(client, &amp;query) {\n            Ok(stmts) =&gt; {\n                let mut stmt_group = Vec::with_capacity(stmts.len());\n                let mut stmt_err = None;\n                for StatementParseResult { ast: stmt, sql } in stmts {\n                    if let Err(err) = check_prohibited_stmts(sender, &amp;stmt) {\n                        stmt_err = Some(err);\n                        break;\n                    }\n                    stmt_group.push((stmt, sql.to_string(), vec![]));\n                }\n                stmt_groups.push(stmt_err.map(Err).unwrap_or_else(|| Ok(stmt_group)));\n            }\n            Err(e) =&gt; stmt_groups.push(Err(e)),\n        },\n        SqlRequest::Extended { queries } =&gt; {\n            for ExtendedRequest { query, params } in queries {\n                match parse(client, &amp;query) {\n                    Ok(mut stmts) =&gt; {\n                        if stmts.len() != 1 {\n                            return Err(Error::Unstructured(anyhow!(\n                                \"each query must contain exactly 1 statement, but \\\"{}\\\" contains {}\",\n                                query,\n                                stmts.len()\n                            )));\n                        }\n\n                        let StatementParseResult { ast: stmt, sql } = stmts.pop().unwrap();\n                        stmt_groups.push(\n                            check_prohibited_stmts(sender, &amp;stmt)\n                                .map(|_| vec![(stmt, sql.to_string(), params)]),\n                        );\n                    }\n                    Err(e) =&gt; stmt_groups.push(Err(e)),\n                };\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    for stmt_group_res in stmt_groups {",

        "contents": "    let runtime = Arc::new(\n        tokio::runtime::Builder::new_multi_thread()\n            .worker_threads(ncpus_useful)\n            .thread_stack_size(3 * 1024 * 1024) // 3 MiB\n            // The default thread name exceeds the Linux limit on thread name\n            // length, so pick something shorter.\n            .thread_name_fn(|| {\n                static ATOMIC_ID: AtomicUsize = AtomicUsize::new(0);\n                let id = ATOMIC_ID.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed);\n                format!(\"tokio:work-{}\", id)\n            })\n            .enable_all()\n            .build()?,\n    );\n\n    // Configure tracing to log the service name when using the process\n    // orchestrator, which intermingles log output from multiple services. Other\n    // orchestrators separate log output from different services.\n    args.tracing.log_prefix = if matches!(args.orchestrator, OrchestratorKind::Process) {\n        Some(\"environmentd\".to_string())\n    } else {\n        None\n    };\n\n    let metrics_registry = MetricsRegistry::new();\n    let tracing_handle = runtime.block_on(args.tracing.configure_tracing(\n        StaticTracingConfig {\n            service_name: \"environmentd\",\n            build_info: BUILD_INFO,\n        },\n        metrics_registry.clone(),\n    ))?;\n    register_runtime_metrics(\"main\", runtime.metrics(), &amp;metrics_registry);\n\n    let span = tracing::info_span!(\"environmentd::run\").entered();\n\n    info!(\"startup: envd init: beginning\");\n    info!(\"startup: envd init: preamble beginning\");\n\n    let metrics = Metrics::register_into(&amp;metrics_registry, BUILD_INFO);\n\n    runtime.block_on(mz_alloc::register_metrics_into(&amp;metrics_registry));\n    runtime.block_on(mz_metrics::register_metrics_into(\n        &amp;metrics_registry,\n        mz_dyncfgs::all_dyncfgs(),\n    ));\n\n    // Initialize fail crate for failpoint support\n    let _failpoint_scenario = FailScenario::setup();\n\n    // Configure connections.\n    let tls = args.tls.into_config()?;\n    let frontegg = FronteggAuthenticator::from_args(args.frontegg, &amp;metrics_registry)?;\n    let listeners_config: ListenersConfig =",

        "contents": "    let tls = args.tls.into_config()?;\n    let frontegg = FronteggAuthenticator::from_args(args.frontegg, &amp;metrics_registry)?;\n    let listeners_config: ListenersConfig = {\n        let f = File::open(args.listeners_config_path)?;\n        serde_json::from_reader(f)?\n    };\n\n    for (_, listener) in &amp;listeners_config.sql {\n        listener\n         "contents": "        task::spawn(|| format!(\"{}_sql_server\", label), {\n            let sql_server = mz_pgwire::Server::new(mz_pgwire::Config {\n                label,\n                tls,\n                adapter_client,\n                authenticator,\n                metrics,\n                active_connection_counter,\n                helm_chart_version,\n                allowed_roles: self.config.allowed_roles,\n            });\n            mz_server_core::serve(ServeConfig {\n                conns: self.connection_stream,\n                server: sql_server,\n                // &#96;environmentd&#96; does not currently need to dynamically\n                // configure graceful termination behavior.\n                dyncfg: None,\n            })\n        });\n        self.handle\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Listener&lt;HttpListenerConfig&gt; {\n    #[instrument(name = \"environmentd::serve_http\")]\n    pub async fn serve_http(self, config: HttpConfig) -&gt; ListenerHandle {\n        let task_name = format!(\"{}_http_server\", &amp;config.source);\n        task::spawn(|| task_name, {\n            let http_server = HttpServer::new(config);\n            mz_server_core::serve(ServeConfig {\n                conns: self.connection_stream,\n                server: http_server,\n                // &#96;environmentd&#96; does not currently need to dynamically\n                // configure graceful termination behavior.\n                dyncfg: None,\n            })\n        });\n        self.handle\n    }\n}\n\npub struct Listeners {\n    pub http: BTreeMap&lt;String, Listener&lt;HttpListenerConfig&gt;&gt;,\n    pub sql: BTreeMap&lt;String, Listener&lt;SqlListenerConfig&gt;&gt;,\n}\n\nimpl Listeners {\n    pub async fn bind(config: ListenersConfig) -&gt;",
